reforms.description: Korea has significantly increased its investment in R&D, reaching 2.9% of GDP by 2001, which is the fifth highest in the OECD. This investment is primarily funded by the business sector, contributing to a substantial rise in patent applications.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased R&D funding enhances innovation capacity, leading to higher productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Korea's R&D investment reached 2.9% of GDP by 2001.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in Korea's approach to R&D funding, significantly enhancing the country's innovation landscape and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: Korea also devotes a relatively large share of national income to R&D investment. By 2001, it had reached 2.9% of GDP, the fifth highest in the OECD area.

reforms.description: The Korean government announced plans to invest 400 billion won in ten industrial sectors identified as new growth engines, focusing on the development of technology, manpower, and infrastructure necessary for these sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Targeted investment in high-potential sectors is expected to stimulate innovation and productivity, contributing to overall economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced plans to invest in ten industrial sectors as new growth engines.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant shift in government policy towards targeted industrial development, aiming to foster innovation and economic growth in key sectors.
reforms.source_quote: In August 2003, the government announced ten industrial sectors as new growth engines, in which it plans to invest 400 billion won in 2004.

reforms.description: The government plans to increase R&D investment in science and technology to 5% of total government outlays, reflecting a commitment to boost public funding for research and development.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased R&D investment is expected to enhance innovation capacity and productivity, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The increase in spending is planned as part of the government's budget for 2004.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in public investment priorities, aiming to substantially enhance the country's R&D capabilities and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: The largest spending increases are planned for R&D investment, as part of the objective of boosting such outlays to 5% of total government outlays.

reforms.description: The government plans to expand tax incentives to promote investment and increase financial assistance to small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) and venture businesses, while helping them secure workers.
reforms.component_name: Tax Incentives for SMEs and Ventures
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing financial assistance and tax incentives, the reform aims to stimulate investment and job creation in innovative sectors, enhancing overall economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announced a plan in 2003 to reform the property holding tax, which includes expanding tax incentives.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant effort to enhance support for SMEs and venture businesses, which are crucial for innovation and job creation.
reforms.source_quote: The authorities will expand tax incentives to promote investment and increase financial assistance to small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) and venture businesses.

reforms.description: The Korean government is concentrating resources in ten strategic industries identified as future growth engines, with plans to invest 400 billion won in 2004 to develop necessary technology, manpower, and infrastructure.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By focusing on strategic industries, the initiative aims to enhance competitiveness in high-technology fields, potentially leading to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government plans to invest 400 billion won in 2004 for the development of these areas.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in resource allocation towards strategic industries, which is crucial for enhancing Korea's competitiveness in the global high-tech market.
reforms.source_quote: The authorities plan to invest 400 billion won in 2004 for the development of these areas.

reforms.description: The reform aims to improve the R&D framework by better utilizing universities and enhancing links between government research institutes, business R&D centers, and universities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing collaboration between academia and industry is expected to boost innovation and productivity, leading to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The recommendation to improve the R&D framework was made as part of the overall assessment and scope for further action.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant effort to enhance the R&D ecosystem by fostering collaboration, which is crucial for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Improve the R&D framework by better utilising the universities and by enhancing the links between government research institutes, business R&D centres and the universities.

reforms.description: Government expenditures on basic research, primarily conducted in universities, increased from 19 percent of the total R&D budget in 2002 to 21 percent in 2004, with plans to rise further to 25 percent by 2007.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for basic research enhances knowledge creation, which is essential for long-term innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Government expenditures on basic research increased as planned.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant increase in funding for basic research, which is crucial for fostering innovation in the long term.
reforms.source_quote: Government expenditures on basic research...increased from 19 percent of the total R&D budget in 2002 to 21 percent in 2004.

reforms.description: The government has introduced numerous cooperation programmes to encourage interaction between R&D in universities, the private sector, and government research institutes, including preferential treatment of the private sector in national R&D funding.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating collaboration between sectors enhances the transfer of knowledge and technology, which can lead to increased innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has introduced numerous cooperation programmes.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly enhances the interaction between R&D sectors, which is vital for fostering innovation and improving competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: The government has introduced numerous cooperation programmes to encourage interaction between R&D in universities, the private sector, and government research institutes.

reforms.description: To attract foreign R&D investment, a special committee was created in the Office of the President in 2004, and the establishment of foreign R&D centers in Korea is an example of strengthened international linkages.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Attracting foreign R&D investment enhances local innovation capabilities and contributes to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: A special committee was created to attract foreign R&D investment.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to enhance Korea's position in the global R&D landscape, fostering innovation and economic development.
reforms.source_quote: To attract foreign R&D investment, a special committee was created in the Office of the President in 2004.
